title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
content_en: The Prophet said, “He claims that when he returns to Madinah, the most honourable one would expel the meanest.” Usayd upon hearing this became enraged and said, “In that case, you, O Messenger of Allah, would expel him if you like! He is, by Allah, the mean person and you are honourable!” Then Usayd attempted to lighten the Prophet’s mood and said, “O Messenger of Allah! Be gentle with him. When Allah brought you to us, his people were decorating the pearls to crown him. He now sees that you have usurped his kingdom.” The Prophet  kept silent and carried on with the journey, whilst some people were still collecting their belongings and others were mounting their rides. The news began to spread until it became the subject of discussion amongst the entire army. “Why are we setting off at this time? What did he say? How should we deal with him? Ibn Salul spoke the truth! No he lied!” were the rumours spreading among the people like Chinese whispers. The army was left confused as they were on their way back from the battle. Moreover some hostile tribes lay in wait for them. The Prophet  felt that the army was about to divide, thus he decided to keep them busy with something else to divert their attention away from the crisis or argumentation, as they continued to inﬂame the matter and create trouble between the The keys to mistakes… Muhajirin and the Ansar. The people began to wait for the moment they would stop over, get together and discuss the matter. Thus, the Prophet  continued to march with the army for the whole day in the sun. He continued until the sun set and the people thought that now they would stop over to pray and rest. But the Prophet  stopped over very brieﬂy, during which they prayed and then moved on again. They continued with their journey throughout the night until they reached the next morning. He stopped over to pray the Fajr prayer and ordered everyone to continue with the journey. They continued to walk the entire morning until they became exhausted and irritated by the sun. When the Prophet  realised that they were completely exhausted and tired such that they were unable to speak, he ordered them to stop over. They had only touched the ground when they all fell asleep. The Prophet  had done this to divert their attention away from the crisis. He then woke them up and continued on the journey until they entered Madinah. The people dispersed and went to their families. Allah then revealed Surat al-Munaﬁqoon: “Those are they who say, ‘spend not on those who are with the Messenger of Allah until they disperse! where as the treasures of the heavens and the earth are only for Allah, but the hypocrites understand not. They say, ‘if we return to Madinah, the one most honourable will surely expel therefrom the one most mean, whereas the honour is for Allah, and His Messenger and the Muslims, but the hypocrites know not.” The Prophet  recited the verses, then took the young boy, Zayd bin Arqam by his ears and said, “He is the one who fulﬁlled The keys to mistakes… his trust to Allah by his ears.” The people then began to insult and blame Ibn Salul. At this point, the Prophet  turned to ‘Umar and said, “Do you see, O ‘Umar? If I were to have killed him on the day you suggested, many would have come to his defence. If today you were to ask me to kill him, I would have killed him.” The Prophet  then remained silent and didn’t say anything further. Sometimes, if a person makes a mistake publicly, then you may need to criticise him for it but in an appropriate manner, even if it be in public. Once the Prophet  was sitting with his Companions, whilst the people were enduring famine, lack of rain and drought. There came a Bedouin and said, “‘O Messenger of Allah, people are being destroyed, the children are hungry, wealth has perished. So ask Allah to send us rain, for we seek your intercession with Allah, and Allah’s intercession with you!” The Prophet’s facial expressions changed upon hearing the man say, “We seek Allah’s intercession with you!” for intercession and mediation is only sought of someone of a lower status with one of a higher status. Hence, it is not permissible to say, “Allah intercedes on behalf of His creation”. Rather, He orders them because He is far superior to all else. The Prophet  said, “Woe be to you! Do you know what you are saying?” The Prophet then began to glorify Allah and kept repeating, “SubhanAllah! SubhanAllah!” He continued to glorify Allah until the seriousness of the matter was noticed in the face of his Companions.
title_id: 5. Maukah Kamu Menemuinya di Luar Lembaga? (lanjutan)
content_id: Rasulullah bersabda, "Ia mengklaim bahwa ketika ia kembali ke Madinah, orang yang paling mulia pasti akan mengusir orang yang paling hina." Usaid mendengar hal ini lalu menjadi berang dan berkata, "Kalau begitu, kamu, wahai Rasulullah, yang akan mengusirnya jika kamu mau! Ia, demi Allah, adalah orang yang hina sedangkan engkau adalah orang yang mulia!" Kemudian Usaid berusaha meringankan suasana hati Rasulullah dan berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah! Bersabarlah dengannya. Ketika Allah membawamu kepada kami, kaumnya sedang menghias mutiara untuk dimahkotakan kepadanya. Ia sekarang melihat bahwa engkau telah merampas kerajaannya." Rasulullah tetap diam dan melanjutkan perjalanan, sementara sebagian orang masih mengumpulkan barang-barang mereka dan yang lain sedang menaiki kendaraan mereka. Berita itu mulai menyebar hingga menjadi bahan pembicaraan di seluruh pasukan. "Mengapa kita berangkat pada waktu seperti ini? Apa yang ia katakan? Bagaimana kita seharusnya menghadapinya? Ibnu Salul berbicara benar! Tidak, ia berdusta!" begitulah desas-desus yang menyebar di antara orang-orang bagaikan bisikan yang saling bersambung. Pasukan pun kebingungan dalam perjalanan pulang dari peperangan. Terlebih lagi, beberapa suku musuh sedang mengintai mereka. Rasulullah merasakan bahwa pasukan hampir terpecah, sehingga beliau memutuskan untuk menyibukkan mereka dengan sesuatu yang lain untuk mengalihkan perhatian mereka dari krisis atau pertengkaran tersebut, karena hal itu terus memperparah masalah dan menimbulkan perselisihan antara kaum Muhajirin dan kaum Anshar. Orang-orang mulai menunggu saat mereka akan singgah, berkumpul bersama, dan membahas masalah tersebut. Maka Rasulullah terus menggerakkan pasukan sepanjang hari di bawah sinar matahari. Beliau terus berjalan hingga matahari terbenam dan orang-orang mengira kini mereka akan singgah untuk salat dan beristirahat. Namun Rasulullah hanya singgah sebentar, melaksanakan salat, lalu bergerak lagi. Mereka melanjutkan perjalanan sepanjang malam hingga tiba di pagi hari. Beliau singgah untuk melaksanakan salat Subuh dan memerintahkan semua orang untuk melanjutkan perjalanan. Mereka terus berjalan sepanjang pagi hingga mereka kelelahan dan terasa berat oleh panas matahari. Ketika Rasulullah menyadari bahwa mereka benar-benar kelelahan dan tidak berdaya hingga tidak mampu berbicara, beliau memerintahkan mereka untuk singgah. Mereka baru saja menyentuh tanah ketika semuanya langsung tertidur. Rasulullah melakukan ini untuk mengalihkan perhatian mereka dari krisis. Kemudian beliau membangunkan mereka dan melanjutkan perjalanan hingga mereka memasuki Madinah. Orang-orang berpencar dan pergi kepada keluarga mereka. Allah kemudian menurunkan Surat al-Munafiqun: "Mereka itulah yang berkata, 'Janganlah kamu menginfakkan harta kepada orang-orang yang ada di sisi Rasulullah sehingga mereka bubar!' Padahal milik Allah-lah perbendaharaan langit dan bumi, tetapi orang-orang munafik itu tidak memahami. Mereka berkata, 'Sesungguhnya jika kita telah kembali ke Madinah, pastilah orang yang kuat akan mengusir orang-orang yang lemah darinya.' Padahal kekuatan itu hanyalah bagi Allah, bagi Rasul-Nya, dan bagi orang-orang mukmin, tetapi orang-orang munafik itu tidak mengetahui." Rasulullah membacakan ayat-ayat itu, lalu memegang telinga anak muda, Zaid bin Arqam, dan berkata, "Dialah yang telah memenuhi amanahnya kepada Allah melalui telinganya." Orang-orang pun mulai menghina dan mencela Ibnu Salul. Saat itu, Rasulullah berpaling kepada Umar dan berkata, "Apakah kamu melihat, wahai Umar? Seandainya aku membunuhnya pada hari yang kamu sarankan, banyak orang yang akan membela. Seandainya hari ini kamu memintaku untuk membunuhnya, aku akan membunuhnya." Rasulullah kemudian diam dan tidak berkata apa-apa lagi. Terkadang, jika seseorang melakukan kesalahan di hadapan publik, maka kamu mungkin perlu mengkritiknya, namun dengan cara yang tepat, sekalipun dilakukan di depan umum. Suatu ketika Rasulullah sedang duduk bersama para sahabatnya, sementara orang-orang sedang mengalami kelaparan, kekurangan hujan, dan kekeringan. Datanglah seorang Badui dan berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, orang-orang sedang binasa, anak-anak kelaparan, harta telah habis. Maka mintalah kepada Allah agar menurunkan hujan kepada kami, karena kami memohon syafaatmu kepada Allah, dan syafaat Allah kepadamu!" Ekspresi wajah Rasulullah berubah mendengar laki-laki itu berkata, "Kami memohon syafaat Allah kepadamu!" karena syafaat dan perantaraan hanya dimohonkan dari orang yang lebih rendah kepada orang yang lebih tinggi. Oleh karena itu, tidak diperbolehkan untuk berkata, "Allah memberikan syafaat demi makhluk-Nya." Melainkan Dia memerintahkan mereka karena Dia jauh lebih tinggi dari segalanya. Rasulullah bersabda, "Celaka kamu! Tahukah kamu apa yang kamu katakan?" Rasulullah kemudian mulai mengagungkan Allah dan terus mengulang, "Subhanallah! Subhanallah!" Beliau terus mengagungkan Allah hingga keseriusan masalah ini terlihat di wajah para sahabatnya.
content_ar: يواصل المؤلف استعراض الوسائل العملية لتحسين العلاقات الإنسانية، مُسلّطًا الضوء على موضوعات: الاعتراف بالخطأ، وحسن الظن بالآخرين، وفضل الصبر وضبط النفس، وكيفية البعد عن الحرص والطمع. ويدعّم كلامه بقصص من حياة النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم والصحابة رضي الله عنهم.
